title: A resolution supporting the goals and ideals of "Countering International Parental Child Abduction Month" and expressing the sense of the Senate that Congress should raise awareness of the harm caused by international parental child abduction.
plain_summary: This resolution supports making April 2025 a special month to teach people about international parental child abduction. This happens when one parent illegally takes their child to another country without permission from the other parent. Thousands of American children have been taken this way, which causes serious emotional harm to both the children and the parents left behind. The Senate wants to keep working to educate people about this problem and help stop these abductions from happening.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations. (text: CR S2097)

sections.title: Resolution Supporting Countering International Parental Child Abduction Month
sections.text: The Senate supports the goals of Countering International Parental Child Abduction Month. The Senate believes Congress should help people understand the harm caused when parents take their children to other countries without permission from the other parent. Thousands of children have been taken from the United States by one parent, separating them from their other parent who stays in America. It is against federal law to take a child out of the United States or keep a child in another country when this prevents the lawful rights of the other parent. Between two thousand ten and two thousand twenty, nine thousand eight hundred sixteen children were reported as abducted from the United States. In two thousand twenty three, cases involving American children were found in one hundred five countries around the world. The United States follows an international agreement called the Hague Convention on Child Abduction that helps return children who were wrongfully taken and protects parental custody rights. Most children who were taken from the United States have not yet been returned to their parents. In two thousand twenty three, sixteen countries were identified as not following proper procedures to return abducted children. These countries include Argentina, Brazil, Egypt, India, Russia, and others. Between two thousand fifteen and two thousand twenty three, nineteen other countries were also found to be non-compliant, showing the need for continued enforcement. The Supreme Court has recognized that family abduction is a form of child abuse that can have devastating consequences for children, including negative impacts on their physical and mental health. Abducted children may experience loneliness, anger, fear of abandonment, anxiety, eating problems, nightmares, mood swings, sleep problems, and aggressive behavior. International parental child abduction causes devastating emotional harm to both the child and the parent left behind. The United States promotes child welfare through government offices including the Children's Bureau and the Office of Children's Issues. Several organizations work to end international parental child abduction and help bring children home. Congress has passed multiple laws to address this issue and the Senate has adopted several resolutions to raise awareness. All fifty states and Washington DC have laws that make parental kidnapping a crime. In two thousand twenty three, the State Department answered more than four thousand six hundred questions from the public about preventing children from being taken out of the country. They also enrolled more than three thousand seven hundred children in a program that alerts officials when someone applies for a passport for that child. This program has enrolled over sixty six thousand six hundred children since it started. The State Department cannot track where a child goes if they travel to a third country after leaving the United States. Children who are American citizens may also have citizenship in other countries and can travel using foreign passports, making it harder to find them and making prevention efforts more important. In two thousand twenty three, two hundred five children were returned to the United States, and one hundred nineteen other cases involving one hundred forty seven children were resolved without the children returning. The Department of Homeland Security worked with the State Department to enroll three hundred forty one children in a program that works with border officers to try to stop children from being taken out of the country. The Senate recognizes and observes Countering International Parental Child Abduction Month from April first through April thirtieth, two thousand twenty five, to raise awareness and opposition to international parental child abduction. The Senate urges the United States to continue leading efforts to educate the public about the harmful emotional, psychological, and physical effects on children and parents who are victims of international parental child abduction.
